title: Hemidesmas plenus
taxonomy: Hemidesmas plenus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the family. Further, Hemidesmas plenus is part of the genus Hemidesmus, which consists of a few species of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. The plant is also classified as belonging to the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that includes other families such as Apocynaceae and Gentianaceae. By classification, Hemidesmas plenus is an evergreen or deciduous shrub or climber.

intro: Hemidesmus indicus, also known as Anantmool or Indian Sarsaparilla, is a climbing plant native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Hemidesmus indicus include saponins, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hemidesmus indicus include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
